name: Amir Mann
tagline: M.Sc. student in Computer Science at the Technion, working on 3D generative AI and human motion generation.
bio: I am an M.Sc. student in Computer Science at the Technion — Israel Institute of Technology, working in LitLab under the advisement of Dr. Or Litany. My research focuses on 3D generative AI and human motion generation from 2D supervision, including alternatives to diffusion supervision and diffusion guidance, working with cameras, SMPL-X, depth, and 3D Gaussian Splatting.

details: LitLab (3D Machine Learning), advised by Dr. Or Litany

education.institution: KAIST — Korea Advanced Institute of Science & Technology

honors.title: 1st in B.Sc. Graduating Class, finished 1 year early
LAPIDIM Excellence Program, Technion CS Innovation Track

research_experience.description: Advised by Dr. Or Litany. Research in 3D generative AI and human motion generation from 2D supervision. Working on alternatives to diffusion supervision and on diffusion guidance. Working with cameras, SMPL-X, depths, 3DGS and more.

publications.title: VideoMDM: Towards 3D Human Motion Generation From 2D Supervision
SpectralSplats: Robust Differentiable Tracking via Spectral Moment Supervision
Time-to-Move: Training-Free Motion Controlled Video Generation via Dual-Clock Denoising

projects.title: Fluid Simulation Tool
projects.description: Rhino/Grasshopper plugin for mesh-based fluid equilibrium simulation. Tool used in research under review at Nature Materials.

projects.title: Genomic Variant Analysis
projects.description: Pipeline (bcftools, GATK, plink, gnomAD) to identify rare mutations in a delayed puberty cohort.

projects.title: Neural Network Robustness Verification
projects.description: Accelerated L0 adversarial robustness verification via regression-based dynamic strategy selection.

other_experience.description: Data analysis across multiple independent sources. Cross-unit project leadership.
Developed SKILLZ 2018, Israel’s national high-school coding competition.
